channel.title: Politics on The Cogitating Ceviche
channel.description: Recent content in Politics on The Cogitating Ceviche

item.title: The Age Gate and the Panopticon Nursery
item.description:
The Cogitating Ceviché is a . , . nowPANOPTICON: A panopticon is a system of control in which people behave as if they are always being watched, whether anyone is actually watching them or not. Its power lies less in constant surveillance than in…

Read the full article on Substack

item.title: The Heat Must Go Somewhere
item.description:
Every serious machine needs a plan for where the heat goes.This is not metaphor. It is not politics. It is not an aesthetic complaint about the soul of modern technology. It is thermodynamics, that impolite branch of reality that keeps showing up…

Read the full article on Substack

item.title: The Small Dominion of the Home
item.description:
The Cogitating Ceviché is a . , . nowModern politics wants every problem scaled upward.Every discomfort becomes a public crisis. Every failure becomes a legislative demand. Every private wound is translated into the language of systems, programs,…

Read the full article on Substack

item.title: The Collapse of the Middle Layer
item.description:
The Cogitating Ceviché is a . , . nowThere was a time, not so long ago, when a local dispute could remain local.This was not because Americans were wiser then. One should never overestimate the political maturity of any people who invented both the…

item.title: John Locke and the Ownership of the Self in a Digital Republic
item.description:
The Cogitating Ceviché is a . , . nowPrefaceFew thinkers have shaped the modern political imagination more deeply than John Locke. His arguments about natural rights, government by consent, private property, education, and the limits of political…

Read the full article on Substack

item.title: James Watt: Power Without Season
item.description:
The Cogitating Ceviché is a . , . nowHe did not invent steam. He solved the problem that kept it from becoming general, and in doing so, helped reorganize the conditions of human work in ways that have not stopped compounding.On a Sunday afternoon…

Read the full article on Substack

item.title: Kitchen-Table Companies:
item.description:
The Old Game Is OverThere used to be a ritual to this sort of thing. You got a badge from an incubator (a startup bootcamp with free desks and dubious mentors), learned to speak venture capital in a minor key, and pitched PowerPoint slides to…

item.title: Remember When I Told You Blockchain Will Power Everything? Well...
item.description:
nowIntroduction: In Which I Admit I Was Being a Bit DramaticA while back, I wrote an article boldly proclaiming that “Blockchain Will Power Everything.” I was about as right as someone predicting the horse-and-buggy would dominate transportation…
